questions.scenario: A sales manager needs access to records owned by direct reports.
questions.question: Which Salesforce feature most directly supports this requirement?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: PSGs aggregate permissions but do not create hierarchical record access.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: OWD establishes baseline access only.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Role hierarchy opens record access upward to managers.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: IP ranges control login, not record access.

questions.explanation: Role hierarchy is the most direct mechanism for upward record access through management structure.

citations.title: Salesforce Help: Control Access to Records
citations.relevanceNote: Explains record access and role hierarchy.

questions.question: Which feature controls the baseline level of access a user has to records they do not own?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Profiles control object and field access, not record-level baseline.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: OWD sets the baseline record access for non-owners.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Permission sets extend object/field access, not record sharing baseline.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Sharing rules open access above OWD but do not set the baseline.

questions.explanation: OWD is the foundation of the record-access pyramid.

citations.title: Trailhead: Data Security
citations.relevanceNote: Module covering OWD and the sharing model.

questions.domain: Object Manager and Lightning App Builder
questions.question: Which field type creates a parent-child relationship that cascades delete?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Lookup is loosely coupled and does not cascade delete by default.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Master-Detail tightly couples the child to the parent and cascades delete.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Hierarchical is a special lookup on the User object.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: External lookup targets an external object.

questions.explanation: Master-Detail enforces parent ownership and cascade delete.

questions.question: Which automation tool is now the recommended option for record-triggered automation?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Workflow Rules are retired for new automations.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Process Builder is also being retired in favor of Flow.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Salesforce recommends Flow for new record-triggered automation.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Apex is only when declarative tools cannot meet a requirement.

questions.explanation: Flow Builder is the strategic automation tool.

citations.title: Salesforce Help: Migrate to Flow
citations.relevanceNote: Guidance on moving off Workflow / Process Builder.

questions.question: Which report format lets you group records by both row and column?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Tabular has no grouping.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Summary groups by rows only.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Matrix groups by both rows and columns.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Joined combines multiple report blocks.

questions.explanation: Matrix reports support row + column grouping.

citations.title: Salesforce Help: Report Formats
citations.relevanceNote: Defines tabular, summary, matrix, and joined formats.

questions.question: Which object represents a qualified sales prospect that is being actively worked?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Leads are unqualified prospects.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Opportunities track qualified deals in the pipeline.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Accounts represent organizations.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Cases are service tickets.

questions.question: Which feature automatically routes incoming cases based on defined criteria?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Assignment rules route cases to users/queues based on criteria.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Escalation rules act on idle cases.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Auto-response sends emails, not routes.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Validation rules enforce data quality.

questions.explanation: Assignment rules are the routing mechanism.

questions.question: Which feature lets users follow records and receive feed updates?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Chatter feeds support following records, users, and groups.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Tasks are activities, not feeds.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Events are calendar items.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Notes attach to records but are not feeds.

questions.question: Which tool is best for bulk-loading 50,000 Contact records with parent Account lookups?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Import Wizard handles up to 50k but with limited object support and no full upsert features.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Data Loader handles large volumes and most standard/custom objects with relationships.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Schema Builder is for designing objects, not loading data.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Object Manager configures metadata.

questions.explanation: Data Loader is preferred for large bulk loads.

questions.domain: Object Manager and Lightning App Builder
questions.question: Which builder allows admins to drag and drop Lightning components onto a record page?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Flow Builder builds automation, not page layouts.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Schema Builder models data, not pages.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Lightning App Builder is the drag-and-drop tool for record/app/home pages.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Page Layout Editor edits Classic-style layouts.

questions.explanation: Lightning App Builder is the modern page composer.
